title: From Substack to YouTube, here are the social platforms cracking down on AI slop
byline: Sydney Bradley, Katherine Li, Dan Whateley
excerpt: Platforms like YouTube and TikTok are navigating the fine line between embracing AI and low-quality, machine-generated content.
content_html:
By Sydney Bradley

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

, Katherine Li

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

,and Dan Whateley

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

As tech companies race to weave AI into every inch of their products, they're discovering there's a fine line between embracing new technology and flooding users with low-quality, machine-generated content.

The shifting guidelines reflect a broader tension across the creator economy. Not long ago, social platforms competed aggressively for influencers with creator funds, exclusive programs, and monetization tools. Many of those same companies are now investing billions in AI products and infrastructure, leaving some creators worried they're becoming less central to the platforms they helped grow.

Here's how major social platforms that rely on creators, including YouTube and Substack, are dealing with the proliferation of AI content.

YouTube

YouTube is sharpening its stance on AI-generated content.

In an update from the company's trust and safety team, on July 20, YouTube said it wants to reward creators who use AI to enhance original storytelling, as opposed to those who mass-produce generic content or AI personas designed to game the platform.

A YouTube spokesperson told Business Insider that the policy isn't new but an important clarification of YouTube's long-standing "inauthentic content" policy, intended to reduce ambiguity for content creators who have been demonetized for repetitive, low-effort, or emotionally manipulative videos.

YouTube has spent years pitching itself to advertisers as the future of television, and a surge of AI-generated spam threatens to undermine that message. In January, the platform removed more than a dozen popular channels that had amassed millions of views by pumping out AI-generated videos featuring characters like cats and Jesus. Video editing platform Kapwing published a report estimating that 21% of YouTube's feed was AI-generated videos.

TikTok

TikTok isn't shying away from generative AI. The company and its owner, ByteDance, offer an array of AI tools for content creators on its marquee app and video editor CapCut. Creators who post on its e-commerce platform, TikTok Shop, have been ramping up the use of AI to sell products this year. Much of the company's tech is built around ByteDance's AI video model, Seedance 2.0.

Still, TikTok is taking a variety of steps to keep generative AI under control across its ecosystem. The company requires creators to label AI-generated content and appends an invisible watermark to the metadata of some videos to indicate they are artificially generated. In November, the company introduced the ability for users to toggle the amount of AI content that appears in their feeds. In July, the company said it's beginning to test "improved detection systems to target accounts dedicated to posting AI-generated spam that crowds out original creators."

Substack

The newsletter platform announced on July 21 that it would help readers identify AI-generated content.

Substack will now let readers scan content to estimate how much AI may have been used in its writing. Substack is partnering with Pangram, an AI detector tool, to power its AI identification tools.

Creators on Substack can also add a disclosure to their content explaining how they made it, such as noting that they used Claude for copyediting, or if they don't use AI at all.

However, users can fairly easily disable this tool for their Substack content by turning off AI analysis in their publication settings. Creators can opt out of Pangram's automated scanning, meaning readers will no longer see an AI-use estimate on those posts.

Pinterest

Pinterest is no stranger to AI slop.

In 2025, the platform began adding AI labels to content. Pinterest lets creators label content as AI-modified or -generated, and has its own detection systems to flag content that may have been AI-generated.

Pinterest also added a tool that lets users limit the amount of AI-generated content appearing in their feeds.

In June, Pinterest's CEO Bill Ready told Business Insider's editor in chief, Jamie Heller, at Cannes Lions that while there are "so many great uses of AI," there is also a "bit of a counter movement to that with consumers."

"We provide users with controls to decide whether they want to see more or less GenAI content in their feed," a Pinterest spokesperson told Business Insider. "Additionally, our Community Guidelines apply to GenAI content, and our recommendation system prioritizes high-quality content, regardless of if it's human-created or GenAI."

Meta

Meta wants users to create and engage with content made with its own AI tools, so, for the most part, it has let content like the famed Shrimp Jesus run free.

However, the company has been labeling content as AI-generated since 2024. Across Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, Meta adds an "AI info" label to content it detects as AI-generated. It doesn't automatically do the same for content that was modified with AI, though. Meta "may require" its AI labels on content with "photorealistic video or realistic-sounding audio that was digitally created, modified or altered, including with AI," per the company's help page explaining its AI policies. People can also voluntarily add the label to their content.

In June, Meta started putting its "AI info" label on advertisements that use AI, whether they're created by Meta's own tools or third-party editing services.

LinkedIn

If your LinkedIn posts sound a little too polished, readers may be taking notice.

AI detector Pangram estimates that 41% of long-form LinkedIn posts and 30% of short-form posts from April through June were AI-generated, which is the highest share among platforms it analyzed, including X, Reddit, Substack, and Medium.

Some creators are already changing course. Steven Bartlett's media company, FlightStory, stopped using AI-written LinkedIn posts after concluding authentic, human-written updates performed better.

LinkedIn actively promotes AI writing tools, including a button that helps users polish posts with AI. The company, however, said in a June post that it will be taking steps against "slop."

"While AI can be a helpful tool for refining language, we're seeing a rise in what many call 'AI slop,' content that is low-effort, AI-generated content that may sound polished on the surface but lacks any real unique perspective or substance," the company wrote.

Sydney Bradley

Sydney Bradley has been covering media and tech for Business Insider since 2020. She breaks news and writes extensively about Instagram and Facebook, as well as new platforms and startups shaping social media, dating apps, the creator economy, venture capital, and tech culture.Sydney's reporting on Instagram was nominated as a finalist for the 2021 Los Angeles Press Club National Entertainment Journalism Awards.She graduated from the University of Virginia with a degree in American Studies. You can follow Sydney's work on LinkedIn, Twitter, and Instagram at @sydneykbradley.Have a tip? You can also contact her via encrypted messaging app Signal (@sydneykbradley.123), encrypted email (sydneykbradley@proton.me), or standard email (sbradley@businessinsider.com). sentences.en: Many of those same companies are now investing billions in AI products and infrastructure, leaving some creators worried they're becoming less central to the platforms they helped grow.

blocks.html_en: YouTube

blocks.html_en: In an update from the company's trust and safety team, on July 20, YouTube said it wants to reward creators who use AI to enhance original storytelling, as opposed to those who mass-produce generic content or AI personas designed to game the platform.

sentences.zh: 在公司信任和安全团队 7 月 20 日的更新中，YouTube 表示，它希望奖励那些使用人工智能来增强原创故事讲述的创作者，而不是那些大量生产通用内容或旨在博弈平台的人工智能角色的创作者。

blocks.html_en: A YouTube spokesperson told Business Insider that the policy isn't new but an important clarification of YouTube's long-standing "inauthentic content" policy, intended to reduce ambiguity for content creators who have been demonetized for repetitive, low-effort, or emotionally manipulative videos.

sentences.zh: YouTube 发言人告诉 Business Insider，该政策并不是什么新鲜事，而是对 YouTube 长期存在的“不真实内容”政策的重要澄清，旨在减少那些因重复、省力或情感操纵视频而被非货币化的内容创作者的歧义。

blocks.html_en: Creators on Substack can also add a disclosure to their content explaining how they made it, such as noting that they used Claude for copyediting, or if they don't use AI at all.

sentences.en: Creators on Substack can also add a disclosure to their content explaining how they made it, such as noting that they used Claude for copyediting, or if they don't use AI at all.
sentences.zh: Substack 上的创作者还可以在其内容中添加披露信息，解释其制作方式，例如指出他们使用 Claude 进行文案编辑，或者他们是否根本不使用人工智能。

blocks.html_en: However, users can fairly easily disable this tool for their Substack content by turning off AI analysis in their publication settings. Creators can opt out of Pangram's automated scanning, meaning readers will no longer see an AI-use estimate on those posts.

sentences.en: However, users can fairly easily disable this tool for their Substack content by turning off AI analysis in their publication settings.
sentences.zh: 但是，用户可以通过在其发布设置中关闭 AI 分析来相当轻松地对其 Substack 内容禁用此工具。

sentences.en: Creators can opt out of Pangram's automated scanning, meaning readers will no longer see an AI-use estimate on those posts.
sentences.zh: 创作者可以选择退出 Pangram 的自动扫描，这意味着读者将不再看到这些帖子的人工智能使用估计。

blocks.html_en: Pinterest

sentences.en: Pinterest
sentences.zh: 兴趣

blocks.html_en: Pinterest is no stranger to AI slop.

sentences.en: Pinterest is no stranger to AI slop.
sentences.zh: Pinterest 对 AI 垃圾并不陌生。

blocks.html_en: In 2025, the platform began adding AI labels to content. Pinterest lets creators label content as AI-modified or -generated, and has its own detection systems to flag content that may have been AI-generated.

sentences.en: In 2025, the platform began adding AI labels to content.
sentences.zh: 2025年，该平台开始为内容添加人工智能标签。

sentences.en: Pinterest lets creators label content as AI-modified or -generated, and has its own detection systems to flag content that may have been AI-generated.
sentences.zh: Pinterest 让创作者将内容标记为人工智能修改或生成，并拥有自己的检测系统来标记可能是人工智能生成的内容。

blocks.html_en: Pinterest also added a tool that lets users limit the amount of AI-generated content appearing in their feeds.

sentences.en: Pinterest also added a tool that lets users limit the amount of AI-generated content appearing in their feeds.
sentences.zh: Pinterest 还添加了一个工具，可以让用户限制人工智能生成的内容出现在他们的提要中的数量。

blocks.html_en: In June, Pinterest's CEO Bill Ready told Business Insider's editor in chief, Jamie Heller, at Cannes Lions that while there are "so many great uses of AI," there is also a "bit of a counter movement to that with consumers."

sentences.en: In June, Pinterest's CEO Bill Ready told Business Insider's editor in chief, Jamie Heller, at Cannes Lions that while there are "so many great uses of AI," there is also a "bit of a counter movement to that with consumers."
sentences.zh: 6 月，Pinterest 首席执行官比尔·雷迪 (Bill Ready) 在戛纳国际创意节上对 Business Insider 的主编杰米·海勒 (Jamie Heller) 表示，虽然“人工智能有如此多的伟大用途”，但也存在“一些与消费者相反的运动”。

blocks.html_en: "We provide users with controls to decide whether they want to see more or less GenAI content in their feed," a Pinterest spokesperson told Business Insider. "Additionally, our Community Guidelines apply to GenAI content, and our recommendation system prioritizes high-quality content, regardless of if it's human-created or GenAI."

sentences.en: "We provide users with controls to decide whether they want to see more or less GenAI content in their feed," a Pinterest spokesperson told Business Insider.
sentences.zh: Pinterest 发言人告诉《商业内幕》：“我们为用户提供控制功能，让他们决定是否想在自己的推送中看到更多或更少的 GenAI 内容。”

sentences.en: "Additionally, our Community Guidelines apply to GenAI content, and our recommendation system prioritizes high-quality content, regardless of if it's human-created or GenAI."
sentences.zh: “此外，我们的社区准则适用于 GenAI 内容，我们的推荐系统会优先考虑高质量内容，无论它是人类创建的还是 GenAI 的。”

blocks.html_en: Meta
Meta wants users to create and engage with content made with its own AI tools, so, for the most part, it has let content like the famed Shrimp Jesus run free.

sentences.en: Meta wants users to create and engage with content made with its own AI tools, so, for the most part, it has let content like the famed Shrimp Jesus run free.
sentences.zh: Meta 希望用户创建并参与使用自己的人工智能工具制作的内容，因此，在很大程度上，它让像著名的虾耶稣这样的内容自由运行。

blocks.html_en: However, the company has been labeling content as AI-generated since 2024. Across Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, Meta adds an "AI info" label to content it detects as AI-generated. It doesn't automatically do the same for content that was modified with AI, though. Meta "may require" its AI labels on content with "photorealistic video or realistic-sounding audio that was digitally created, modified or altered, including with AI," per the company's help page explaining its AI policies. People can also voluntarily add the label to their content.

sentences.en: However, the company has been labeling content as AI-generated since 2024.
sentences.zh: 然而，自 2024 年以来，该公司一直将内容标记为人工智能生成。

sentences.en: Across Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, Meta adds an "AI info" label to content it detects as AI-generated.
sentences.zh: 在 Facebook、Instagram 和 Threads 中，Meta 为其检测为人工智能生成的内容添加了“人工智能信息”标签。

sentences.en: It doesn't automatically do the same for content that was modified with AI, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，它不会自动对使用人工智能修改的内容执行相同的操作。

sentences.en: Meta "may require" its AI labels on content with "photorealistic video or realistic-sounding audio that was digitally created, modified or altered, including with AI," per the company's help page explaining its AI policies.
sentences.zh: 根据该公司解释其人工智能政策的帮助页面，Meta“可能要求”其人工智能标签包含“以数字方式创建、修改或改变的逼真视频或逼真音频，包括人工智能”。

sentences.en: People can also voluntarily add the label to their content.
sentences.zh: 人们还可以自愿将标签添加到他们的内容中。

blocks.html_en: In June, Meta started putting its "AI info" label on advertisements that use AI, whether they're created by Meta's own tools or third-party editing services.

sentences.en: In June, Meta started putting its "AI info" label on advertisements that use AI, whether they're created by Meta's own tools or third-party editing services.
sentences.zh: 今年 6 月，Meta 开始在使用人工智能的广告上贴上“人工智能信息”标签，无论这些广告是由 Meta 自己的工具还是第三方编辑服务创建的。

blocks.html_en: LinkedIn

sentences.en: LinkedIn
sentences.zh: 领英

blocks.html_en: If your LinkedIn posts sound a little too polished, readers may be taking notice.

sentences.en: If your LinkedIn posts sound a little too polished, readers may be taking notice.
sentences.zh: 如果你的 LinkedIn 帖子听起来有点过于优雅，读者可能会注意到。

blocks.html_en: AI detector Pangram estimates that 41% of long-form LinkedIn posts and 30% of short-form posts from April through June were AI-generated, which is the highest share among platforms it analyzed, including X, Reddit, Substack, and Medium.

sentences.en: AI detector Pangram estimates that 41% of long-form LinkedIn posts and 30% of short-form posts from April through June were AI-generated, which is the highest share among platforms it analyzed, including X, Reddit, Substack, and Medium.
sentences.zh: AI 检测器 Pangram 估计，从 4 月到 6 月，41% 的长篇 LinkedIn 帖子和 30% 的短篇帖子是 AI 生成的，这是其分析的平台（包括 X、Reddit、Substack 和 Medium）中所占比例最高的。

blocks.html_en: Some creators are already changing course. Steven Bartlett's media company, FlightStory, stopped using AI-written LinkedIn posts after concluding authentic, human-written updates performed better.

sentences.en: Some creators are already changing course.
sentences.zh: 一些创作者已经在改变路线。

sentences.en: Steven Bartlett's media company, FlightStory, stopped using AI-written LinkedIn posts after concluding authentic, human-written updates performed better.
sentences.zh: 史蒂文·巴特利特 (Steven Bartlett) 的媒体公司 FlightStory 在得出真实的、人工编写的更新效果更好后，停止使用人工智能编写的 LinkedIn 帖子。

blocks.html_en: LinkedIn actively promotes AI writing tools, including a button that helps users polish posts with AI. The company, however, said in a June post that it will be taking steps against "slop."

sentences.en: LinkedIn actively promotes AI writing tools, including a button that helps users polish posts with AI.
sentences.zh: LinkedIn积极推广人工智能写作工具，其中包括一个帮助用户用人工智能润色帖子的按钮。

sentences.en: The company, however, said in a June post that it will be taking steps against "slop."
sentences.zh: 然而，该公司在 6 月份的一篇文章中表示，将采取措施防止“溢出”。

blocks.html_en: "While AI can be a helpful tool for refining language, we're seeing a rise in what many call 'AI slop,' content that is low-effort, AI-generated content that may sound polished on the surface but lacks any real unique perspective or substance," the company wrote.

sentences.en: "While AI can be a helpful tool for refining language, we're seeing a rise in what many call 'AI slop,' content that is low-effort, AI-generated content that may sound polished on the surface but lacks any real unique perspective or substance," the company wrote.
sentences.zh: 该公司写道：“虽然人工智能可以成为改进语言的有用工具，但我们看到许多人所说的‘人工智能废品’的内容有所增加，这些内容是人工智能生成的省力内容，表面上听起来很优美，但缺乏任何真正独特的视角或实质内容。”

blocks.html_en: Sydney Bradley has been covering media and tech for Business Insider since 2020. She breaks news and writes extensively about Instagram and Facebook, as well as new platforms and startups shaping social media, dating apps, the creator economy, venture capital, and tech culture.Sydney's reporting on Instagram was nominated as a finalist for the 2021 Los Angeles Press Club National Entertainment Journalism Awards.She graduated from the University of Virginia with a degree in American Studies. You can follow Sydney's work on LinkedIn, Twitter, and Instagram at @sydneykbradley.Have a tip? You can also contact her via encrypted messaging app Signal (@sydneykbradley.123), encrypted email (sydneykbradley@proton.me), or standard email (sbradley@businessinsider.com). Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device; here’s our guide to sharing information securely.Selected stories:

sentences.en: She breaks news and writes extensively about Instagram and Facebook, as well as new platforms and startups shaping social media, dating apps, the creator economy, venture capital, and tech culture.Sydney's reporting on Instagram was nominated as a finalist for the 2021 Los Angeles Press Club National Entertainment Journalism Awards.She graduated from the University of Virginia with a degree in American Studies.
sentences.zh: 她发布了有关 Instagram 和 Facebook 的新闻并发表了大量文章，以及塑造社交媒体、约会应用程序、创作者经济、风险投资和科技文化的新平台和初创公司。悉尼在 Instagram 上的报道被提名为 2021 年洛杉矶新闻俱乐部国家娱乐新闻奖决赛入围者。她毕业于弗吉尼亚大学，获得美国研究学位。

blocks.html_en: • Artificial Intelligence

blocks.html_en: • More

content_html_zh:
作者：Sydney Bradley 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 , Katherine Li 您正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。和 Dan Whateley 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

随着科技公司竞相将人工智能融入其产品的每一寸，他们发现，拥抱新技术和向用户灌输低质量的机器生成内容之间存在着微妙的界限。

指导方针的变化反映了创作者经济中更广泛的紧张局势。 不久前，社交平台凭借创作者资金、独家项目和货币化工具，激烈争夺影响者。 其中许多公司现在正在人工智能产品和基础设施上投资数十亿美元，这让一些创作者担心，他们在自己帮助发展的平台中的地位正在下降。

以下是依赖创作者的主要社交平台（包括 YouTube 和 Substack）如何应对人工智能内容的激增。

YouTube

YouTube 正在强化其对人工智能生成内容的立场。

在公司信任和安全团队 7 月 20 日的更新中，YouTube 表示，它希望奖励那些使用人工智能来增强原创故事讲述的创作者，而不是那些大量生产通用内容或旨在博弈平台的人工智能角色的创作者。

YouTube 发言人告诉 Business Insider，该政策并不是什么新鲜事，而是对 YouTube 长期存在的“不真实内容”政策的重要澄清，旨在减少那些因重复、省力或情感操纵视频而被非货币化的内容创作者的歧义。

YouTube 多年来一直向广告商宣传自己是电视的未来，而人工智能生成的垃圾邮件的激增可能会破坏这一信息。 一月份，该平台删除了十多个热门频道，这些频道通过播放人工智能生成的以猫和耶稣等角色为主角的视频而积累了数百万次观看次数。 视频编辑平台 Kapwing 发布的一份报告估计，YouTube 的 21% 的内容是人工智能生成的视频。

抖音

TikTok 并不回避生成式人工智能。 该公司及其所有者字节跳动在其招牌应用程序和视频编辑器 CapCut 上为内容创作者提供了一系列人工智能工具。 今年，在其电子商务平台 TikTok Shop 上发帖的创作者一直在加大力度使用人工智能来销售产品。 该公司的大部分技术都是围绕字节跳动的人工智能视频模型 Seedance 2.0 构建的。

尽管如此，TikTok 仍在采取各种措施来控制整个生态系统中的生成式人工智能。 该公司要求创作者对人工智能生成的内容进行标记，并在某些视频的元数据中附加隐形水印，以表明它们是人工生成的。 去年 11 月，该公司推出了让用户可以切换出现在提要中的人工智能内容数量的功能。 今年 7 月，该公司表示，它开始测试“改进的检测系统，以专门发布人工智能生成的垃圾邮件、排挤原创者的账户为目标”。

子栈

该时事通讯平台于 7 月 21 日宣布，将帮助读者识别人工智能生成的内容。

Substack 现在可以让读者扫描内容来估计其写作中可能使用了多少人工智能。 Substack 正在与 AI 检测工具 Pangram 合作，为其 AI 识别工具提供支持。

Substack 上的创作者还可以在其内容中添加披露信息，解释其制作方式，例如指出他们使用 Claude 进行文案编辑，或者他们是否根本不使用人工智能。

但是，用户可以通过在其发布设置中关闭 AI 分析来相当轻松地对其 Substack 内容禁用此工具。 创作者可以选择退出 Pangram 的自动扫描，这意味着读者将不再看到这些帖子的人工智能使用估计。

兴趣

Pinterest 对 AI 垃圾并不陌生。

2025年，该平台开始为内容添加人工智能标签。 Pinterest 让创作者将内容标记为人工智能修改或生成，并拥有自己的检测系统来标记可能是人工智能生成的内容。

Pinterest 还添加了一个工具，可以让用户限制人工智能生成的内容出现在他们的提要中的数量。

6 月，Pinterest 首席执行官比尔·雷迪 (Bill Ready) 在戛纳国际创意节上对 Business Insider 的主编杰米·海勒 (Jamie Heller) 表示，虽然“人工智能有如此多的伟大用途”，但也存在“一些与消费者相反的运动”。

Pinterest 发言人告诉《商业内幕》：“我们为用户提供控制功能，让他们决定是否想在自己的推送中看到更多或更少的 GenAI 内容。” “此外，我们的社区准则适用于 GenAI 内容，我们的推荐系统会优先考虑高质量内容，无论它是人类创建的还是 GenAI 的。”

Meta

Meta 希望用户创建并参与使用自己的人工智能工具制作的内容，因此，在很大程度上，它让像著名的虾耶稣这样的内容自由运行。

然而，自 2024 年以来，该公司一直将内容标记为人工智能生成。 在 Facebook、Instagram 和 Threads 中，Meta 为其检测为人工智能生成的内容添加了“人工智能信息”标签。 不过，它不会自动对使用人工智能修改的内容执行相同的操作。 根据该公司解释其人工智能政策的帮助页面，Meta“可能要求”其人工智能标签包含“以数字方式创建、修改或改变的逼真视频或逼真音频，包括人工智能”。 人们还可以自愿将标签添加到他们的内容中。

今年 6 月，Meta 开始在使用人工智能的广告上贴上“人工智能信息”标签，无论这些广告是由 Meta 自己的工具还是第三方编辑服务创建的。

领英

如果你的 LinkedIn 帖子听起来有点过于优雅，读者可能会注意到。

AI 检测器 Pangram 估计，从 4 月到 6 月，41% 的长篇 LinkedIn 帖子和 30% 的短篇帖子是 AI 生成的，这是其分析的平台（包括 X、Reddit、Substack 和 Medium）中所占比例最高的。

一些创作者已经在改变路线。 史蒂文·巴特利特 (Steven Bartlett) 的媒体公司 FlightStory 在得出真实的、人工编写的更新效果更好后，停止使用人工智能编写的 LinkedIn 帖子。

LinkedIn积极推广人工智能写作工具，其中包括一个帮助用户用人工智能润色帖子的按钮。 然而，该公司在 6 月份的一篇文章中表示，将采取措施防止“溢出”。

该公司写道：“虽然人工智能可以成为改进语言的有用工具，但我们看到许多人所说的‘人工智能废品’的内容有所增加，这些内容是人工智能生成的省力内容，表面上听起来很优美，但缺乏任何真正独特的视角或实质内容。”

阅读下一篇
